Wen Hui, styledManji, was fromQi[祁],Taiyuan[太原].
When his father, [Wen] Shu, who was theGrand AdministratorofZhuo commandery[涿郡太守], died,[Wen] Huiwas fifteen. He took part in the funeral procession that returned to his homevillage.1•••
Wen Huiescorted the corpse of his father back to their hometown for it to be buried.↩
His household had sufficient wealth.[Wen] Huisaid, “The world is about to be embroiled in chaos. What do I need wealth for?” In one day, he had distributed it all, generously giving it to his clansmen. His hometown thought of him highly and compared him to Xun Yue [郇越].2•••
Xun Yue, styled Chenzhong, was a man who lived during the late WesternHan dynasty. He gave millions of his wealth that he inherited from his ancestors to his clansmen, up to the ninth degree.↩
He was nominated asFilial and Incorrupt. He served asChiefofLinqiu[廩丘長],PrefectofYanling[鄢陵令],PrefectofGuangchuan[廣川令],ChancellorofPengcheng[彭城相], andChancellorofLu[魯相]. Wherever he was, he was praised. He entered to serve asMaster of Recordsunder theAssisting Chancellor, then left to beInspectorofYang province[揚州刺史]. TheGrand Progenitorsaid, “I very m...
